name: Associateships

links.label: Supreme and District Court
links.modalContent: Applications open in December, approximately 14 months before commencement. Apply directly to an individual judge OR via a general pool. https://courts.qld.gov.au/about/jobs-with-the-courts/judges-associates

links.label: Land Court
links.modalContent: Application Form + Questionnaire + cover letter (1 page max) + CV (5 pages max with 2 referees) + transcript. Submit to the Executive Assistant. https://courts.qld.gov.au/courts/land-court/land-court-associates

links.label: QCAT
links.modalContent: Applications open and close in August. Required: Application Form + Cover letter + CV + transcript. Submit to qcattribunal.appointments@justice.qld.gov.au.

links.label: QIRC
links.modalContent: Vacancies advertised on SmartJobs and Seek. https://www.qirc.qld.gov.au/

links.label: Federal Court of Australia
links.modalContent: Apply to an individual judge. Associateships are appointed 1 to 2 years in advance, typically in January or February. query@fedcourt.gov.au · https://www.fedcourt.gov.au/about/employment/associates

orgs.name: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia

links.label: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia
links.modalContent: Recruitment via the Court's online recruitment system. Vacancies advertised on APSJobs (https://www.apsjobs.gov.au/).

links.label: Administrative Review Tribunal
links.modalContent: Recruitment via the ART Career Opportunities portal. recruitment@art.gov.au · (02) 9276 5443 · https://www.art.gov.au/about-us/careers

links.label: Fair Work Commission
links.modalContent: Own job portal. Associates support approximately 55 Members. Roles advertised at APS5 level for 12 to 18 months (extendable to 2 years). Merit pool valid for 18 months from date of advertisement.

bodyContent:
## What is an Associate?

A Judge's Associate assists a judge or court. In Australia, an Associate is typically a recent graduate or a lawyer assisting a specific judge. The role also extends to Tribunal Members at the Administrative Appeals Tribunal (AAT), QCAT, and the Fair Work Commission.

## What does Associateship involve?

Associateship is the privilege of working with experienced judges as mentors. The role involves frequent attendance at court, observation of oral advocacy, and exposure to judicial decision-making.

Daily tasks include: assisting in court, summarising matters, proofreading judgments, liaising with parties, and conducting legal research. The role often includes circuit travel and extra-judicial tasks such as function planning and speechwriting.

Career benefits flow into innumerable fields - many alumni cite it as the single most formative year of their early career.

## How to Apply?

Application processes vary per Court and Tribunal. Generally, you'll need a CV, a cover letter, and an academic transcript. An interview with the judge or member is typical for shortlisted candidates.
